html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 涉异志 - Title of the text, can be translated as 'Involving Strange Records' or 'Concerning Strange Records.'
  • 罗源紫霄岩 - Refers to Zixiao Rock in Luoyuan, a specific location in Fujian Province.
  • 石真妃 - Refers to the two goddesses, Stone True Consorts, venerated at Zixiao Rock.
  • 永乐初出海 - Refers to the early period of the Yongle Emperor's reign, marked by significant maritime activities.
  • 戴星过岭 - Literally 'wearing stars over the mountains,' a poetic way of saying traveling at night.
  • 于归之妇 - Refers to a woman returning to her husband's home after marriage, a traditional Chinese term.
  • 弛担候之 - Means to set down one's burden and wait for the procession.
  • 叩谢而过 - Means to bow and thank before passing by.
  • 其初罗源徐公里石氏女也 - Refers to the origin of the two goddesses as daughters of the Xu family in Luoyuan.
  • 姊曰月华，妹曰雪英 - Refers to the names of the two sisters, Moonlight and Snowy.
  • 皆有姿色，涉书史 - Means they were both beautiful and knowledgeable in literature and history.
  • 五季末 - Refers to the late period of the Five Dynasties (907-960), a time of political fragmentation.
  • 处州青巾贼作乱 - Refers to a rebellion by the Green Turban rebels in Chuzhou, a region in Zhejiang.
  • 义不受辱 - Means they chose to die rather than be dishonored.
  • 相继投河死 - Means they threw themselves into the river one after the other and died.
  • 宋时林孝子戆孙入山采樵遇玉女 - Refers to a story from the Song dynasty where a filial son named Gao Zun encountered a jade maiden while gathering firewood in the mountains.
  • 明妆俨然 - Means she was dressed in a clear and dignified manner.
  • 肃入其家 - Means she entered his home respectfully.
  • 延茶久之 - Means she invited him to drink tea for a long time.
  • 世乱年荒起盗兵 - Means the world is in chaos, the years are barren, and bandits have risen up.
  • 纷纷蝼蚁尚逃生 - Means even the ants are trying to escape, emphasizing the desperation of the times.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石真妃 - The two goddesses, Stone True Consorts, venerated at Zixiao Rock and believed to have divine powers.
  • 永乐初出海 - The early period of the Yongle Emperor's reign, marked by significant maritime expeditions and exploration.
  • 处州青巾贼作乱 - The Green Turban Rebellion, a significant uprising during the late Five Dynasties period.
  • 宋时林孝子戆孙入山采樵遇玉女 - A story from the Song dynasty that highlights the moral and spiritual significance of encountering a divine figure.
  • 月华吟曰 - The poem attributed to Moonlight, reflecting the turmoil and suffering of the times.

Proper Nouns

  • 石真妃 - The two goddesses, Stone True Consorts.
  • 罗源紫霄岩 - Zixiao Rock in Luoyuan.
  • 永乐 - The reign title of Emperor Yongle.
  • 处州 - Chuzhou, a region in Zhejiang.
  • 青巾贼 - Green Turban rebels.
  • 宋 - The Song dynasty.
  • 林孝子 - A filial son from the Song dynasty.
  • 戆孙 - Gao Zun, the filial son.
  • 玉女 - Jade maiden, a divine figure.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妾身不幸遭俘虏 - Phrase expressing personal misfortune and captivity.
  • 雨涕何时积恨平 - Poetic expression of tears and accumulated resentment.
  • 百尺潺溪探禹穴 - Metaphorical reference to exploring deep or hidden places, possibly alluding to the legendary achievements of Yu the Great.
  • 寸心皎洁付陶泓 - Poetic expression of purity and integrity, possibly referring to a symbolic act of dedication.
  • 皇天不泯坚贞女 - Phrase expressing divine recognition of steadfastness and virtue.
  • 召拜云阶浪得名 - Phrase expressing the idea of receiving a prestigious position or title.
  • 昔日繁华若转蓬 - Metaphorical expression of the transient nature of prosperity.
  • 千玑万琲总成空 - Poetic expression of the ultimate futility or emptiness of material wealth.
  • 肉芝胜比莲花鲊 - Metaphorical comparison, possibly referring to the value of spiritual or moral virtues over material wealth.
  • 甘露何如竹叶醲 - Poetic expression of the superiority of spiritual nourishment over material indulgence.
  • 物外烟霞随处得 - Metaphorical expression of finding peace and beauty in the world beyond material concerns.
  • 世间风雨任牢笼 - Poetic expression of enduring worldly hardships.
  • 知君已有曾参行 - Reference to the virtuous deeds of Zeng Shen, a disciple of Confucius.
  • 暂与寻常一径通 - Phrase expressing the idea of a temporary return to ordinary life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雪英 - The name of the speaker, likely a character in a literary work.
  • 石氏女 - Refers to a woman from the Shi family, possibly a historical or legendary figure.
  • 上帝悯吾贞烈 - Reference to divine recognition of steadfastness and virtue.
  • 火部曜灵真妃 - Title or rank, possibly a deity or a spiritual being associated with fire.
  • 水部风毒真妃 - Title or rank, possibly a deity or a spiritual being associated with water.
  • 紫霄岩 - Place name, possibly a sacred or significant location.
  • 石八娘岩 - Another name for the same place, possibly a local or regional name.
  • 戆孙 - The name of the listener, likely a character in a literary work.
  • 曾参行 - Reference to the virtuous deeds of Zeng Shen, a disciple of Confucius.

Proper Nouns

  • 雪英 - The name of the speaker.
  • 石氏女 - The name of the woman from the Shi family.
  • 上帝 - The divine being.
  • 火部曜灵真妃 - Title or rank.
  • 水部风毒真妃 - Title or rank.
  • 紫霄岩 - Place name.
  • 石八娘岩 - Another name for the same place.
  • 戆孙 - The name of the listener.
  • 曾参 - A historical figure, a disciple of Confucius.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 浮梁东隅有昭烈庙祠 - Literally means 'In the eastern corner of Fuliang, there is a temple dedicated to Emperor Zhaolie.' '东隅' (eastern corner) is a specific location, and '昭烈庙祠' refers to a temple dedicated to Emperor Zhaolie, who is likely Liu Bei (刘备), a famous figure from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 唐张巡设像旁 - '唐张巡' refers to Zhang Xun, a general from the Tang dynasty known for his bravery and loyalty. '设像旁' means 'placed an image beside.'
  • 侍曰张太子 - '侍曰' means 'serving as' or 'referred to as.' '张太子' is a title given to Zhang Xun, possibly due to his status or actions.
  • 永乐戊子 - '永乐' is the era name of Emperor Yongle of the Ming dynasty, and '戊子' is a stem-branch year notation, specifically the 35th year of the Yongle era (1438).
  • 士人卜秋举 - '士人' means 'scholar' or 'scholar-official,' and '卜秋举' means 'divined for the autumn examination.'
  • 降箕曰 - '降箕' refers to the divination method using a divining board or similar device. '曰' means 'said.'
  • 玉霄一点坠云端 - A poetic expression meaning 'a single point of jade falling from the clouds,' often used metaphorically to describe something precious or rare.
  • 难失佳人一不全 - '难失' means 'difficult to lose' or 'hard to miss,' and '佳人一不全' means 'a beautiful woman is incomplete,' suggesting a metaphorical or symbolic meaning.
  • 敲断凤钗文不就 - '敲断' means 'knocked off,' '凤钗' is a phoenix-shaped hairpin, and '文不就' means 'the text is not complete' or 'the writing is unfinished.'
  • 贵人头上请君看 - '贵人' means 'noble person' or 'important person,' and '头上请君看' means 'please look at the top of the head,' which could be a metaphor for a high position or status.
  • 英山东滨州人 - '英' is the name of the person, '山东' means 'Shandong province,' and '滨州' means 'Binzhou city.'
  • 其父斌为浮梁令 - '斌' is the father's name, and '浮梁令' means 'magistrate of Fuliang.'
  • 以父任应举云 - '以父任' means 'due to his father's position,' and '应举' means 'to take the examination.'
  • 士人袖芭蕉叶入云 - '袖芭蕉叶' means 'carried a banana leaf in his sleeve,' and '入云' means 'asked in the cloud,' referring to a form of divination.
  • 我非问功名 - '我非' means 'I am not,' and '问功名' means 'asking about success in the imperial examination.'
  • 第言袖中何物 - '第言' means 'just say,' and '袖中何物' means 'what is in my sleeve.'
  • 两手怀来一叶清 - '两手怀来' means 'with both hands,' '一叶清' means 'a single clear leaf,' possibly referring to a metaphorical or symbolic object.
  • 知君无意问功名 - '知君' means 'know you,' and '无意问功名' means 'do not intend to ask about success in the imperial examination.'
  • 可怜昨夜三更雨 - '可怜' means 'pitiful' or 'regrettable,' and '昨夜三更雨' means 'last night's rain at the third watch of the night.'
  • 减却窗前数点声 - '减却' means 'reduced,' '窗前' means 'in front of the window,' and '数点声' means 'a few sounds,' possibly referring to the sound of raindrops.
  • 士人得异草来问其名 - '异草' means 'strange grass,' and '问其名' means 'ask for its name.'
  • 苏武当年胆气雄 - '苏武' is a historical figure known for his loyalty, and '胆气雄' means 'brave and resolute.'
  • 匈奴一箭射飞鸿 - '匈奴' refers to the Xiongnu, an ancient nomadic people, and '一箭射飞鸿' means 'shot a flying wild goose with an arrow,' a metaphor for a heroic act.
  • 至今血染阶前草 - '至今' means 'until now,' '血染' means 'stained with blood,' and '阶前草' means 'grass in front of the steps.'
  • 一度秋来一度红 - '一度' means 'once' or 'each time,' and '秋来' means 'in autumn,' '红' means 'red,' suggesting a cyclical or recurring theme.
  • 或谓是时有旅衬寓庙中 - '或谓' means 'some say,' '是时' means 'at that time,' '有旅衬' means 'there was a traveler,' and '寓庙中' means 'staying in the temple.'
  • 必其所为 - '必' means 'certainly,' and '其所为' means 'what he did.'
  • 后衬移而神不显 - '后' means 'after,' '衬移' means 'the traveler moved,' and '神不显' means 'the spirit did not appear.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浮梁 - A county in present-day Jiangxi province, known for its tea production.
  • 昭烈庙祠 - A temple dedicated to Emperor Zhaolie, likely referring to Liu Bei (刘备), a famous figure from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 张巡 - A general from the Tang dynasty known for his bravery and loyalty, particularly during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 张太子 - A title given to Zhang Xun, possibly due to his status or actions.
  • 永乐戊子 - The 35th year of the Yongle era (1438) of the Ming dynasty.
  • 王英 - A historical figure who passed the imperial examination.
  • 山东滨州 - A city in Shandong province.
  • 浮梁令 - The magistrate of Fuliang, a position held by Wang Ying's father.
  • 苏武 - A historical figure known for his loyalty to the Han dynasty, famously refusing to submit to the Xiongnu.
  • 雁落红 - A name given to a strange plant, possibly due to its appearance or properties.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 布衣时贫甚 - Refers to a commoner who is extremely poor.
  • 患其无以自给 - Means to worry about being unable to support oneself.
  • 盟曰 - Means to swear an oath or make a solemn promise.
  • 发身即完婚 - Means to enter public service and get married immediately.
  • 厉坛荒僻 - Refers to a desolate and remote temple or shrine.
  • 攒棺甚众 - Means piled-up coffins, very many.
  • 人不敢近 - Means people dare not approach.
  • 富家少年戏曰 - Means a wealthy young man jokingly said.
  • 厉坛第七棺中贮钱三百文 - Means the seventh coffin in the temple contains three hundred coins.
  • 秀才今夜能取之乎 - Means can the scholar take it tonight?
  • 公许诺 - Means the person agreed.
  • 少年以饼十四枚畀之 - Means the wealthy young man gave him fourteen pieces of cake.
  • 使分置各棺以为验 - Means to place separately in each coffin as proof.
  • 至夜 - Means until night.
  • 群鬼相语 - Means a group of ghosts talking to each other.
  • 戚侍郎来此，盍出避，惜此人不获令终耳 - Means Grand Secretary Qi comes here, why don't you leave and avoid, pity this person won't have a good end.
  • 已而公至 - Means soon after the person arrived.
  • 群鬼辟易匿榛莽间 - Means the ghosts scatter and hide among the bushes.
  • 公分置饼各棺 - Means the person places the cakes separately in each coffin.
  • 取钱而去 - Means to take the money and leave.
  • 恬然无惧 - Means calmly without fear.
  • 明日，少年贺公曰 - Means the next day, the wealthy young man congratulated the person.
  • 秀才位至侍郎也，但当慎晚节耳 - Means the scholar reaches the position of Grand Secretary, but should be cautious in later years.
  • 后登进士完婚 - Means later passes the imperial examination and gets married.
  • 累官工侍 - Means gradually promoted to Grand Secretary of the Ministry of Works.
  • 以同邑贺侍郎谮 - Means because slandered by another Grand Secretary He from the same county.
  • 果弃市 - Means indeed was executed in public.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 戚侍郎 - Refers to Grand Secretary Qi, a high-ranking official.
  • 布衣 - Refers to a commoner or someone without official status.
  • 厉坛 - A desolate and remote temple or shrine.
  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar or student, often used to describe someone who has passed the preliminary level of the imperial examinations.
  • 进士 - Refers to those who have passed the highest level of the imperial examinations.
  • 工侍 - Refers to the Grand Secretary of the Ministry of Works, a high-ranking official.
  • 同邑贺侍郎 - Refers to another Grand Secretary He from the same county.
  • 弃市 - Refers to public execution, a form of capital punishment.

Proper Nouns

  • 戚侍郎 - Grand Secretary Qi.
  • 秀才 - Scholar or student.
  • 进士 - Imperial examination passers.
  • 工侍 - Grand Secretary of the Ministry of Works.
  • 同邑贺侍郎 - Grand Secretary He from the same county.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黄■〈鱼曷〉道 - A term referring to a specific type of fish or a fish-related phenomenon. The character '■〈鱼曷〉' is unclear and may be a typo or rare character. Translated as 'a certain type of fish' or 'a fish phenomenon.'
  • 一切蠢动咸有佛性 - Means 'all creatures, no matter how simple, possess Buddha-nature.'
  • 未几病剧 - Means 'soon fell gravely ill.'
  • 恍惚见道人携其类万数索命 - Means 'vaguely saw a Taoist with countless others demanding his life.'
  • 遂死 - Means 'and died.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宣德四年 - The fourth year of the Xuande era (1429) during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 宁德 - A prefecture in Fujian Province, China.
  • 督银场 - Refers to the supervision of a silver mine.
  • 太监 - A eunuch, a castrated male who served in the imperial court.
  • 毒鱼 - Refers to the practice of poisoning fish, which was sometimes used in traditional Chinese medicine or as a method of pest control.
  • 佛性 - Refers to the inherent potential for enlightenment in all beings, a concept from Buddhism.
  • 道人 - Refers to a Taoist practitioner or hermit.
  • 索命 - Means 'to demand one's life,' often associated with supernatural or ghostly phenomena in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 周觉成 - The name of the eunuch who oversaw the silver mine in Ningde.
  • 宁德 - The name of the prefecture in Fujian Province.
  • 宣德 - The era name of the Ming Dynasty emperor Xuande.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 下第 - Refers to failing the imperial examinations, a common idiom in classical Chinese literature.
  • 四鼓 - Means 'four drums' or 'four watches of the night,' referring to the early hours of the morning.
  • 甲第 - Refers to passing the highest level of the imperial examinations, achieving the rank of jinshi (进士).
  • 仕为郡 - Means 'serving as a prefect or magistrate,' indicating a high-ranking official position.
  • 终参藩 - Means 'eventually becoming a governor or provincial administrator,' indicating a senior administrative role.
  • 拘罪人 - Means 'to arrest a criminal,' indicating a legal action.
  • 山麓 - Refers to the foot of a mountain.
  • 破晓 - Means 'dawn' or 'early morning,' emphasizing the time of day.
  • 白日无颜过此 - Means 'ashamed to pass by during the day,' expressing the protagonist's embarrassment.
  • 何虑也 - A rhetorical question meaning 'what to worry about,' indicating reassurance.
  • 平明 - Means 'dawn' or 'early morning,' similar to '破晓.'
  • 阴司 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead, often associated with supernatural beings.
  • 摄人之鬼 - Means 'spirits that capture souls,' indicating supernatural entities.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少参临海范公文初 - Refers to Fan Wenchu, a historical figure from the Ming dynasty, who served as a vice minister (少参) and was from Linhai (临海).
  • 景泰甲戌进士 - Refers to the year 1454, when Fan Wenchu passed the imperial examinations during the reign of Emperor Jingtai (景泰).
  • 甲戌 - The stem-branch calendar system, where '甲戌' corresponds to the year 1454.
  • 关山 - A mountain range, likely referring to a specific location in the story.
  • 新昌 - A place name, possibly a county or city in the region.

Proper Nouns

  • 少参临海范公文初 - Fan Wenchu, a historical figure.
  • 景泰甲戌 - The year 1454, during the reign of Emperor Jingtai.
  • 关山 - A mountain range.
  • 新昌 - A place name.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 罔极之恩 - Refers to the boundless and infinite kindness of one's parents, often used in the context of filial piety.
  • 连理而生 - Describes two trees growing together, symbolizing a harmonious and inseparable relationship.
  • 交股摇动 - Means 'crossed limbs shaking,' describing the physical movement of the trees.
  • 出告反而 - Means 'go out and return,' referring to the ritual of reporting one's activities to ancestors or parents.
  • 事生之义 - Refers to the proper way of treating one's parents while they are alive, emphasizing filial piety and respect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 连江 - Refers to a place in Fujian Province, China, indicating the geographical setting of the story.
  • 倪神保 - The name of the protagonist, a person who lost his parents at a young age and is deeply devoted to filial piety.
  • 林氏 - The name of the protagonist's wife, who shares his devotion and participates in the prayer.
  • 石芹山 - The name of a mountain where the miraculous trees are found, adding a local and specific detail to the story.
  • 刻父母像以祀焉 - The act of carving statues of one's parents and worshipping them, a practice rooted in Chinese filial piety and ancestor worship.

Proper Nouns

  • 倪神保 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 林氏 - The name of the protagonist's wife.
  • 石芹山 - The name of the mountain where the trees are found.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 祷雨文 - A formal document used in rituals to pray for rain, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 景泰五年七月 - Refers to the seventh month of the fifth year of the Jingta era, indicating the historical context of the text.
  • 宁德旱甚 - Literally 'Ningde suffered a severe drought,' describing the situation that prompted the prayer.
  • 邑民祷雨西□□□ - The missing characters likely refer to a specific location or deity associated with rain prayers.
  • 疏文未善 - Means 'the petition was not well-written,' suggesting that the initial prayer was inadequate.
  • 投之须臾 - Means 'after a short time,' indicating the passage of time between the initial and subsequent prayers.
  • 别浮片纸 - Refers to a small piece of paper that floated separately, possibly a note or additional prayer.
  • 元至正间 - Refers to the Yuan Dynasty's Zhizheng era, indicating the historical period of the discovered text.
  • 俯陈蚁悃 - A humble expression meaning 'to humbly present one's sincere heart,' used to show respect in the prayer.
  • 仰渎龙颜 - Literally 'to humbly address the dragon's face,' a metaphor for addressing a deity or authority.
  • 乞鉴凡心 - Means 'to request the deity to understand the petitioner's simple heart.'
  • 愿舒圣眼 - Means 'to hope the deity will open its holy eyes.'
  • 言念生居 - Means 'reflecting on life and residence,' introducing the petitioners' concerns.
  • 在世治本于农 - Means 'the foundation of governance lies in agriculture,' emphasizing the importance of farming.
  • 蒙天地覆载之恩 - Means 'receiving the great favor of heaven and earth,' expressing gratitude.
  • 感雨露滋濡之德 - Means 'appreciating the kindness of rain and dew,' highlighting the importance of water.
  • 春耕夏耨 - Refers to spring plowing and summer weeding, describing agricultural activities.
  • 敢辞涂足之勤 - Means 'not to shirk the hard work of treading soil,' showing dedication to farming.
  • 秋获冬藏 - Refers to autumn harvest and winter storage, describing the agricultural cycle.
  • 实切资身之望 - Means 'truly hoping to support oneself,' expressing the petitioners' practical needs.
  • 不期兹岁忽值恒旸 - Means 'unfortunately, this year unexpectedly faced prolonged heat,' describing the drought.
  • 爰从九夏之初 - Means 'starting from the beginning of the ninth summer,' indicating the duration of the drought.
  • 逮及三秋之始 - Means 'until the beginning of the third autumn,' further specifying the drought's duration.
  • 已经六旬不雨 - Means 'for sixty days without rain,' quantifying the severity of the drought.
  • 忍见千里扬尘 - Means 'unable to bear seeing dust rising over a thousand miles,' describing the dry conditions.
  • 石燕停飞 - A metaphor for the cessation of natural phenomena due to the drought.
  • 商羊绝舞 - Another metaphor for the cessation of natural phenomena due to the drought.
  • 致泉源之既竭 - Means 'leading to the exhaustion of water sources,' describing the impact of the drought.
  • 慨旱魃之为凶 - Means 'lamenting the evil of the drought demon,' personifying the drought as a malevolent force.
  • 知作孽自我丞民 - Means 'knowing that the calamity is due to the sins of the common people,' attributing the drought to human actions.
  • 故降灾实由上帝 - Means 'thus, the disaster is truly caused by the heavenly god,' attributing the drought to divine punishment.
  • 爰深修省 - Means 'thus, deeply reflect and repent,' suggesting a call for self-examination and repentance.
  • 特致祷祈 - Means 'especially offering prayers,' indicating the intensity of the petition.
  • 虔取某时 - Means 'humbly choosing a specific time,' showing respect in the ritual.
  • 恭诣龙湫 - Means 'respectfully going to the dragon pool,' referring to a sacred site for rain prayers.
  • 恪求圣水 - Means 'humbly seeking holy water,' a key part of the ritual.
  • 愿施甘雨 - Means 'hoping for sweet rain,' expressing the petitioners' desire.
  • 稍救枯苗 - Means 'to slightly save the withered crops,' describing the hoped-for outcome.
  • 伏愿龙颜大悦 - Means 'humbly hoping the dragon's face is greatly pleased,' showing respect to the deity.
  • 赐涓滴于金瓶龟拆 - Means 'granting even a drop from the golden vase of the tortoise,' a metaphor for a small but significant amount of rain.
  • 全滋霈滂沱于陇亩 - Means 'fully nourishing the fields with abundant rain,' describing the desired outcome.
  • 庸副实栗之咏 - Means 'to truly fulfill the heartfelt praise,' expressing the petitioners' hope for gratitude.
  • 预占大有之祥 - Means 'to foresee a bountiful harvest,' describing the hoped-for future.
  • 垂佑我民迓续乃命云云 - Means 'bestowing blessings on our people to continue their lives,' concluding the prayer with a plea for divine favor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 景泰五年七月 - The Jingta era (1450-1456) was a period of the Ming Dynasty, indicating the historical context of the initial prayer.
  • 宁德 - A county in Fujian Province, China, providing the geographical context.
  • 元至正间 - The Zhizheng era (1341-1370) was a period of the Yuan Dynasty, indicating the historical period of the discovered text.
  • 龙湫 - A sacred pool or spring associated with dragons, a common site for rain prayers in Chinese culture.
  • 旱魃 - A mythical creature or demon associated with drought, personifying the natural disaster.
